description: Fix empty dashboard (stale cache bug), broken calendar page, missing navigation, cross-session plan persistence, and add task check-off, loading animation, and next-week preview

userStories.title: Fix stale plan cache detection in API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the /api/parse-pdf route to detect and discard cached plans in the old 12-month format so the dashboard always receives valid 52-week data.
acceptanceCriteria: After fetching parsed_plan from the DB cache, check if parsed_plan[0]?.week !== undefined
If the first entry has a 'month' key instead of 'week' key, the plan is old format — skip the cache and regenerate
To skip: do NOT return the cached result; fall through to the Claude API call as if no cache existed
After regeneration, save the new 52-week plan back to DB via the existing ON CONFLICT upsert
If DB is unavailable, fall through to generation as before (no change to existing error handling)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rewrite calendar page for 52-week WeeklyPlan data
userStories.description: As a user, I want my full annual calendar to display the correct week-by-week plan so I can see all my tasks throughout the year.
acceptanceCriteria: Import WeeklyPlan and WeeklyTask types from '@/app/api/parse-pdf/route' (already exported)
Remove all MonthlyPlan / MonthlyTask interface definitions from the file
Add a weekToMonth(week: number): number helper: Math.min(Math.ceil(week / (52 / 12)), 12)
Call /api/parse-pdf with { state, grassType } (no pdfUrl) to get WeeklyPlan[] — same fetch pattern as dashboard
Calendar view tab: show 12 month columns; for each month, collect all WeeklyPlan entries where weekToMonth(week) === monthNum and deduplicate category dots
Clicking a month column expands a panel below showing all tasks from that month's weeks, grouped by week number with a 'Week N' subheading
List view tab: 12 month sections each showing all tasks for that month's weeks as a flat list (no week subheadings in list view)
Current month highlighted in var(--color-primary) border as before
Category color legend at the bottom unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add navigation bar — header links and mobile bottom tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I want to navigate between Dashboard, Calendar, and Profile without typing URLs so I can move freely through the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'use client' directive to src/app/layout.tsx
Import usePathname from 'next/navigation' and Link from 'next/link' in layout.tsx
Move the metadata export (title, description, manifest, themeColor) to a separate src/app/metadata.ts file and import it in layout.tsx as: export { metadata } from './metadata'
Define NAV_ITEMS array: [{ href: '/dashboard', label: 'Home', icon: '🏠' }, { href: '/dashboard/calendar', label: 'Calendar', icon: '📅' }, { href: '/profile', label: 'Profile', icon: '👤' }]
Desktop header (md: and up): render nav links inline in the header div, right-aligned; active link uses var(--color-primary) color and font-semibold; inactive uses var(--color-text-muted)
Mobile bottom tab bar: fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0, height 56px, background var(--color-surface), border-top #2d4a2d, z-index 50; render 3 tab buttons evenly spaced
Each mobile tab shows icon (text-xl) above label (text-xs); active tab icon and label use var(--color-primary); inactive use var(--color-text-muted)
Add pb-16 bottom padding to the <main> element so page content is not hidden behind the bottom tab bar on mobile
Hide bottom tab bar on routes starting with '/onboarding' and on '/' (landing page) — use pathname.startsWith('/onboarding') || pathname === '/' to conditionally render
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Make 'view your full annual calendar' a clickable link
userStories.description: As a user seeing the empty-state message on the dashboard, I want to click the calendar text to navigate there directly.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/dashboard/page.tsx, import Link from 'next/link'
In the empty-state card, split the text so 'view your full annual calendar' is wrapped in <Link href='/dashboard/calendar'>
Link has underline text decoration and color var(--color-primary)
Surrounding text 'Nothing urgent this week — check back soon or ' and '.' remains as plain text
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dashboard cold-load — hydrate from DB when sessionStorage is empty
userStories.description: As a returning user opening a new tab, I want the dashboard to load my plan automatically without redoing onboarding.
acceptanceCriteria: After reading onboarding_state from sessionStorage, check if state.state or state.grass_type is falsy
If either is missing, read lawn_session_id from localStorage
If lawn_session_id exists, dynamically import getProfile from '@/actions/profile' and call getProfile(sessionId)
If the DB profile has state and grass_type, use those values to populate the dashboard (setGrassName, setSqFt, setHasPets) and call /api/parse-pdf
If getProfile returns null or the profile also lacks state/grass_type, set a new React state flag showOnboardingGate = true and skip the API call
If localStorage has no lawn_session_id, set showOnboardingGate = true immediately
Existing behavior (sessionStorage is populated) is unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Onboarding gate — CTA card when no profile exists
userStories.description: As a brand-new user who lands on the dashboard without a profile, I want to see a clear prompt to set up my lawn so I understand what to do.
acceptanceCriteria: Add showOnboardingGate boolean state to src/app/dashboard/page.tsx (set by US-036 logic)

When showOnboardingGate is true and loading is false, render a card in place of the task list containing: heading 'Set up your lawn profile' (font-bold, var(--color-text-primary)), subtext 'Answer a few quick questions to get your personalized 52-week care plan.' (var(--color-text-muted)), and a Link button 'Get started →' pointing to /onboarding/1
Card uses rounded-lg p-6 border with backgroundColor var(--color-surface) and borderColor #2d4a2d
Button uses var(--color-primary) background, var(--color-background) text, rounded-lg py-3 px-6 font-semibold
The 'Right Now' heading and week-range subtitle still render above the card
When showOnboardingGate is false, render nothing extra (existing task list / empty state shown normally)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Plan generation progress animation
userStories.description: As a user waiting for my plan to generate, I want to see descriptive status messages cycling during the load so I know the app is working.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a generatingPlan boolean state to src/app/dashboard/page.tsx; set to true before the /api/parse-pdf fetch begins, set to false after it resolves

While generatingPlan is true, show a loading card below the spinner with a text message that cycles every 3000ms through: 'Identifying your grass type and climate zone…', 'Consulting university extension research…', 'Building your 52-week care calendar…', 'Almost there — personalizing your plan…'
Use setInterval to advance the message index; clear the interval when generatingPlan becomes false or on component unmount
Message text style: text-sm text-center var(--color-text-muted) with mt-4
Only show the cycling messages when generatingPlan is true — never when loading cached data that returns quickly
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Task completion check-off on dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to check off tasks I've finished this week so I can track my progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Add completedTitles: Set<string> state to src/app/dashboard/page.tsx
On mount, read sessionStorage key 'completed_tasks_week_{N}' (where N is the ISO week number) and initialize completedTitles from that JSON array
Each task card header row gets a circular toggle button (w-6 h-6, rounded-full, border 1px #2d4a2d) on the far right of the header
When completedTitles includes the task title: button shows ✓ character, button background var(--color-primary), task title has line-through text decoration, card opacity is 0.6
When not completed: button is empty circle with border #2d4a2d
Clicking the button toggles the task in/out of completedTitles, saves the updated array to sessionStorage as 'completed_tasks_week_{N}', and re-renders
Completed tasks sort to the bottom of the list (after incomplete tasks) — re-sort on every render
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: 'Up Next' next-week preview card on dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a quick preview of next week's tasks so I can plan ahead and buy supplies in time.
acceptanceCriteria: After the current week's task list (or empty state), render an 'Up Next' section only when: the plan has loaded AND the plan contains an entry for week N+1 AND that entry has at least 1 task
Section heading: 'Up Next' (text-sm font-semibold var(--color-text-primary)) followed by the next week's date range (text-xs var(--color-text-muted)) on the same line separated by ' · '
Show up to 3 tasks from next week's entry as a compact list: each item is one line showing the category icon (from CATEGORY_ICONS) + a space + task title (text-sm var(--color-text-muted))
No task descriptions, no priority badges, no pet safety notes in this preview
If next week has more than 3 tasks, append a muted line: '+ N more tasks' where N is the remaining count
Section is wrapped in a rounded-lg p-4 border card with backgroundColor var(--color-surface) and borderColor #2d4a2d
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Profile save syncs sessionStorage and invalidates cached plan
userStories.description: As a user who edits my profile, I want the dashboard to immediately reflect my changes without having to redo onboarding.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/profile/page.tsx, after a successful saveProfile() call, update onboarding_state in sessionStorage: read current value, merge in { state, grass_type: grassType, square_footage: sqFt ? parseInt(sqFt) : null, has_pets: hasPets, sun_exposure: sunExposure }, and write back with JSON.stringify

If the warn flag is true (grass_type or zip changed), also call db.query('DELETE FROM cached_plans WHERE state = $1 AND grass_type = $2', [originalState, originalGrassType]) using the values before the edit — import db from '@/lib/db'
Track originalState alongside existing originalGrassType and originalZip state variables; set it from the loaded profile on mount

After a successful save, show a 'Go to dashboard →' Link (href='/dashboard') below the Save button; use the same style as the Save button but with a transparent/outline variant (border var(--color-primary), text var(--color-primary), background transparent)
The 'Go to dashboard →' link only appears after a successful save (when toast is visible), not before
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
